Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

why is the vaishnava shika not located in the sahasrara chakra?

Health · asked by user [] · 2010-10-14 · 3 answers
... known as "the supreme center of contact with God".
user [154] · 2010-10-14
One is the place that breaks open after you attain samadhi and the other is the little handle used for lifting all others who are otherwise unfit. Good news is that Krishna, unlike some of us, if bhava grahi janardana, he appreciates the good in what he have done and ignores the evil. So we have a chance to be pulled out by Him anyhow.
user [447] · 2010-10-14
Yay! Finally one good reason to be in a female body with hair all over the scalp, so one can be pulled out by any patch of hair!
user [447] · 2010-11-14
[img]http://api.ning.com/files/23VXDxmhCaIJ1QefK2iyu5XhxepldXnmrWvdJBp5i*W*qn-iMH-IWhNgXQjzI1PkqtHqUvaATFgfasNFJL5bVFDpCJK4wFhH/7_06_2009_36.jpg[/img][br][br]

It seems that once men get too bald to wear the sikha at the proper spot, it can be lowered as well.
